@graph.name: Jaguar models fitted with the XK Straight-Six

itemListElement.name: E-Type Series 3 V12 (4.2L not used) — XK6 phase ended in 1971

mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar XK straight-six engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned XK straight-six typically costs £5,000–£7,500 supply only, varying by displacement (3.4L vs 3.8L vs 4.2L) and specification (carburettor vs fuel injection). Full rebuilds by specialists often exceed £10,000.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar XK straight-six a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The XK straight-six earns 4.0★ out of 5 — it's fundamentally well-engineered and robust. Most issues stem from poor maintenance, infrequent use, or incorrect carburettor setup rather than design flaws.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common Jaguar XK straight-six failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain tensioner wear is the most significant concern — the tensioners have small oil passages that can become blocked, reducing pressure on the chain. Rattling on startup is an early warning sign. A tensioner and chain upgrade typically costs £1,200–£2,000.

mainEntity.name: Which Jaguar models use the XK straight-six?

acceptedAnswer.text: The XK6 powered over 20 models across six decades — from the 1949 XK120 sports car through the Mk VII/VIII saloons, Mk 2, E-Type, S-Type, 420/420G, and XJ6 saloons through to 1986.

mainEntity.name: How long does the Jaguar XK straight-six last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With proper maintenance, the XK straight-six is capable of 100,000+ miles before requiring a rebuild. The engine is renowned for its durability — five of the top six cars at the 1957 Le Mans 24 Hours were Jaguar D-Types powered by XK engines.

hero.h1: Jaguar XK Straight-Six Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Jaguar XK straight-six is a legendary DOHC inline-six that powered Jaguar for over four decades — robust and characterful, but timing chain tensioners and carburettor setup need careful attention. Backed by real UK enquiries in 2025.

rows.value: Exclusive to Jaguar (also military: Alvis, Daimler)

verdictRating.confidence: 85% confidence — based on historical production data and specialist rebuild reports.

rows.score: 16 (Robust design; most failures are rebuildable rather than catastrophic)
12 (Specialist parts available but not off-the-shelf; growing remanufacturing support)
16 (Reconditioned cost ~30–44% of lowest classic host-vehicle value)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The XK straight-six is one of the great British engines — durable and rebuildable, but tensioner wear and carburettor setup need specialist care — buy with documented rebuild history.
verdictRating.bestFor: Classic Jaguar enthusiasts who appreciate a robust, characterful engine and don't mind specialist maintenance.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You're looking for a modern, low-maintenance daily driver or don't have access to a specialist.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting a Jaguar XK straight-six typically takes 14–20 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent specialist) — classic Jaguar specialists like Guy Broad or P&K Thornton are recommended.

items.answer: The XK straight-six earns 4.0★ out of 5 — it's fundamentally well-engineered and robust. Most issues stem from poor maintenance, infrequent use, or incorrect carburettor setup rather than design flaws.

items.question: How long does the Jaguar XK straight-six last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, the XK straight-six is capable of 100,000+ miles before requiring a rebuild. The engine is renowned for its durability — five of the top six cars at the 1957 Le Mans 24 Hours were Jaguar D-Types powered by XK engines. Regular servicing and avoiding overheating are key to longevity.
